description: HP 8350B Sweep Oscillator mainframe used with 835xx (83500-series) RF plug-ins. Legacy HP-IB (IEEE-488) instrument using a two-letter mnemonic program-code language (NOT SCPI). Program codes may be upper or lower case; the instrument ignores spaces, plus signs, and unexpected/invalid characters. Numeric entries are not executed until a units terminator (scale suffix) is supplied: frequency GZ/MZ/KZ/HZ (GHz/MHz/kHz/Hz), time SC/MS (seconds/msec), and power dBm/dB. Most parameters that carry a numeric value are read back with the Output Interrogated Parameter function: send 'OP' immediately followed by the function's prefix (e.g. 'OPFA' reads the start frequency); the reply is a 14-character ASCII value of the form +d.dddddE+dd terminated by CR/LF. Output Active Parameter (OA) returns whichever parameter is currently active. Power level, ALC mode and RF on/off control require an 83500-series plug-in and are device dependent on the plug-in installed; HP 86200-series plug-ins cannot be power-programmed via the 8350B. Source: HP 8350B Operating and Service Manual, Table 3-7 HP-IB Program Codes and the per-block Remote Function Procedure tables.

identity.description: Output Software Revision Number / Output Identity. Returns 'O8350B REV X,Y' (mainframe rev X, plug-in rev Y), e.g. 'O8350B REV 1,5', terminated by CR/LF. The 8350B has no SCPI '*IDN?' or '?'-style query; OI is the closest identity query.

commands.name: instrument_preset
commands.description: Instrument Preset. Returns the sweep oscillator to its known preset state (START/STOP mode over the plug-in range, markers off, etc.). SAVE registers are left unchanged.

commands.name: start_frequency
commands.description: Start frequency of the START/STOP swept range. Follow the value with a frequency scale suffix. Range is determined by the installed plug-in. Start must be <= Stop.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: Plug-in dependent (values up to +/-2% out of range are accepted)

commands.name: stop_frequency
commands.description: Stop frequency of the START/STOP swept range. Follow the value with a frequency scale suffix. Range is plug-in dependent. Stop must be >= Start.

commands.name: cw_frequency
commands.description: Continuous-wave (CW) frequency. Selects CW mode and outputs a constant frequency. Resolution 0.00038% of band.

commands.name: swept_cw
commands.description: Swept CW mode ([SHIFT][CW]). Holds a CW frequency while the SWEEP OUT produces a 0 to 10 V ramp, giving a flat horizontal display trace.

commands.name: center_frequency
commands.description: Center frequency of the CF/dF swept range. Output sweeps from CF-dF/2 to CF+dF/2.

commands.name: delta_frequency
commands.description: Delta (span) frequency of the CF/dF swept range (Delta-F frequency span).

commands.name: coarse_cw_resolution
commands.description: Selects coarse CW control-knob resolution ([SHIFT][CF]), 0.0015% of band.

commands.name: fine_cw_resolution
commands.description: Selects fine CW control-knob resolution ([SHIFT][dF]), 0.00038% of band.

commands.name: frequency_vernier
commands.description: CW/center frequency vernier. Adjusts the effective center frequency by up to +/-0.05% of the band in use, resolution 0.00038% of band.

commands.name: frequency_offset
commands.description: Frequency (RF) offset ([SHIFT][VERNIER]). Shifts the CW/center frequency by any amount up to the full plug-in range. Resolution 0.00038% of band.

commands.name: frequency_display_multiplier
commands.description: Frequency display multiplier ([SHIFT][START]). Scales the displayed frequency value (display only; defaults to 1 after Instrument Preset).

commands.name: frequency_display_offset
commands.description: Frequency display offset ([SHIFT][STOP]). Offsets the displayed frequency value (display only; defaults to 0 after Instrument Preset).

commands.name: marker_1
commands.description: Frequency marker 1: selects/positions marker 1 (makes it the active marker). Resolution 0.4% of selected sweep width. Use OPM1 to read its frequency.

commands.name: marker_2
commands.description: Frequency marker 2: selects/positions marker 2 (makes it the active marker).

commands.name: marker_3
commands.description: Frequency marker 3: selects/positions marker 3 (makes it the active marker).

commands.name: marker_4
commands.description: Frequency marker 4: selects/positions marker 4 (makes it the active marker).

commands.name: marker_5
commands.description: Frequency marker 5: selects/positions marker 5 (makes it the active marker).

commands.name: marker_off
commands.description: Turns off the active marker. Sent as a suffix after selecting a marker (M1..M5). The character after M may be the letter O or the digit 0.

commands.name: all_markers_off
commands.description: Turns off all frequency markers ([SHIFT][OFF]).

commands.name: marker_delta
commands.description: Marker delta ([SHIFT][M1]). Displays the frequency difference between the active marker and the previously active marker. Read the difference with OPSHM1.

commands.name: marker_to_center_frequency
commands.description: Marker to Center Frequency. Sets the center frequency to the active marker's frequency, keeping span where possible.

commands.name: marker_sweep
commands.description: Marker sweep on/off. MP1 reassigns markers 1 and 2 to Start and Stop frequencies (M1/M2 sweep on); MP0 turns marker sweep off. Trailing digit selects on (1) or off (0).

commands.name: permanent_marker_sweep
commands.description: Permanent marker sweep ([SHIFT][MKR SWEEP]). Makes the values of markers 1 and 2 the permanent START/STOP frequency values. (Also documented as SHSP in Table 3-7.)

commands.name: counter_interface_enable
commands.description: Counter interface enable ([SHIFT][M2]). Enables swept-frequency counting end points (used with FA, FB, or M1..M5).

commands.name: counter_interface_disable
commands.description: Counter interface disable ([SHIFT][M3]). Disables swept counting.

commands.name: power_level
commands.description: RF output power level (requires 83500-series plug-in; value/range device dependent on the plug-in). Follow the value with a dBm terminator. Read back with OPPL.

commands.name: power_sweep
commands.description: RF power sweep. PS<value> sets the power-sweep magnitude (read with OPPS); PSm form (PS1/PS0) turns power sweep on/off. Requires 83500-series plug-in.

commands.name: power_slope
commands.description: RF power slope. SL<value> sets the slope magnitude (read with OPSL); SLm form (SL1/SL0) turns slope on/off. Compensates power versus frequency. Requires 83500-series plug-in.

commands.name: rf_power
commands.description: RF power output on/off. RF1 turns RF on, RF0 turns RF off. Requires 83500-series plug-in.

commands.name: internal_leveling
commands.description: Selects internal ALC leveling mode (the power-on/preset default ALC mode).

commands.name: external_crystal_leveling
commands.description: Selects external crystal-detector ALC leveling mode.

commands.name: external_power_meter_leveling
commands.description: Selects external power-meter ALC leveling mode.

commands.name: external_sweep
commands.description: External sweep mode. The plug-in oscillator is tuned by a 0 to 10 V ramp applied to the SWP INPUT BNC.

commands.name: manual_sweep
commands.description: Manual sweep mode. Sets a single (manual) frequency point; follow the value with a frequency suffix. Read the present manual frequency with OPSM.

commands.name: sweep_time
commands.description: Sweep time for continuous (time) sweep. Follow the value with a time terminator (SC seconds or MS milliseconds). Minimum sweep time depends on plug-in and bandwidth. Read with OPST.

commands.name: single_sweep
commands.description: Single sweep. Selects/triggers single-sweep mode; initial engagement aborts any sweep in progress (equivalent to trigger T4).

commands.name: trigger_internal
commands.description: Internal sweep trigger (free run / auto). Sweep trigger controls apply only in TIME sweep mode.

commands.name: trigger_line
commands.description: Line sweep trigger (triggered by the power-line frequency).

commands.name: trigger_external
commands.description: External sweep trigger. A >2 V trigger (20 V max), wider than 0.5 us at <1 MHz rep rate, applied to the rear auxiliary connector pin 9.

commands.name: trigger_single
commands.description: Single sweep trigger. Selects/triggers single sweep (same effect as SG / Take Sweep).

commands.name: reset_sweep
commands.description: Reset sweep. Aborts the present single sweep and resets it so it can be triggered again. Valid only in single-sweep trigger mode.

commands.name: take_sweep
commands.description: Take sweep. Triggers a single sweep. Valid only in single-sweep trigger mode.

commands.name: frequency_step_size
commands.description: Frequency step size, used by the step up/down keys for START, STOP, CF, CW, MARKER and MANUAL SWEEP. Follow with a frequency suffix. Not displayed.

commands.name: power_step_size
commands.description: Power step size, used by the step up/down keys when varying POWER LEVEL. Follow with a dBm/dB terminator. Plug-in dependent. Read with OPSP.

commands.name: step_up
commands.description: Step up / increment the active function by the current step size.

commands.name: step_down
commands.description: Step down / decrement the active function by the current step size. (Front panel code DN; the program-code table also lists 'ON' as the OCR rendering of this code.)

commands.name: backspace
commands.description: Backspace. Removes the last entered digit before a units terminator is supplied.

commands.name: default_step_size
commands.description: Reset step sizes to default values ([SHIFT][STEP SIZE]).

commands.name: amplitude_marker
commands.description: Amplitude markers on/off. AK1 puts mainframe frequency markers into RF amplitude dips; AK0 returns them to Z-axis intensity dots.

commands.name: display_blanking
commands.description: Display (CRT) blanking on/off via Z-axis control during retrace. DP1 on, DP0 off.

commands.name: rf_blanking
commands.description: RF blanking on/off. Turns off RF power during the retrace sweep. RP1 on, RP0 off.

commands.name: square_wave_modulation
commands.description: Internal square-wave amplitude modulation on/off. MD1 on, MD0 off. Standard square-wave frequency 27.775 kHz (internally jumper-selectable to 1 kHz).

commands.name: save_register
commands.description: Save current settings into a storage register (1 to 9). Fails with Error 30 if the save lock is engaged.

commands.name: recall_register
commands.description: Recall settings from a storage register (1 to 9). Unused registers contain Instrument Preset settings.

commands.name: memory_lock
commands.description: Save register lock / enable save protection ([SHIFT][SAVE]). Write-protects all registers.

commands.name: memory_unlock
commands.description: Save register unlock / disable save protection ([SHIFT][RECALL]).

commands.name: alternate_sweep
commands.description: Alternate sweep on. Alternates between the current state and a selected stored register (1 to 9) on successive sweeps.

commands.name: alternate_sweep_off
commands.description: Alternate sweep off. (Table 3-7 also lists alternate sweep on/off as the ALmn form.)

commands.name: cw_filter
commands.description: CW filter in/out (83500-series plug-in). FI1 inserts the CW filter, FI0 removes it.

commands.name: fm_sensitivity_20mhz
commands.description: External FM input sensitivity -20 MHz/volt (83500-series plug-in). Reset to the internal switch position at power-on / Instrument Preset.

commands.name: fm_sensitivity_6mhz
commands.description: External FM input sensitivity -6 MHz/volt (83500-series plug-in). Reset to the internal switch position at power-on / Instrument Preset.

commands.name: display_update
commands.description: Display update on/off. DU1 (default) updates the numeric displays on each numeric programming; DU0 blanks the displays to speed up stepped CW programming.

commands.name: network_analyzer_trigger
commands.description: Network analyzer trigger (HP 8410B/C). Generates an external trigger pulse so the 8410B/C re-phase-locks on the present RF signal; the only function valid while the input micro learn string is in effect.

commands.name: output_interrogated_parameter
commands.description: Output Interrogated Parameter. Send 'OP' immediately followed by a function prefix to read that parameter's present numeric value as a 14-character ASCII string (+d.dddddE+dd, CR/LF). Valid prefixes: FA, CW, CF, DF, FB, M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, VR, SHVR, SHM1, SS, ST, SM, PL, PS, SL, SP, SHFA, SHFB.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.range: FA, CW, CF, DF, FB, M1-M5, VR, SHVR, SHM1, SS, ST, SM, PL, PS, SL, SP, SHFA, SHFB

commands.name: output_active_parameter
commands.description: Output Active Parameter. Returns the numeric value of the currently active parameter (enabled for step/data-entry modification) as +d.dddddE+dd, CR/LF.

commands.name: output_harmonic_number
commands.description: Output Harmonic Number. Returns the ratio of RF OUTPUT frequency to AUX OUTPUT frequency as 'dd' (CR/LF); zero in the heterodyne-band condition.

commands.name: output_software_revision
commands.description: Output Software Revision Number (Output Identity). Returns 'O8350B REV X,Y' (mainframe rev X, plug-in rev Y), CR/LF terminated, e.g. 'O8350B REV 1,5'.

commands.name: output_learn_string
commands.description: Output Learn String. Returns a 90-byte binary string fully describing the present instrument/plug-in state (excludes storage registers), terminated with EOI. Restore with IL.

commands.name: input_learn_string
commands.description: Input Learn String. Send 'IL' followed by the 90-byte binary learn string previously read with OL to restore that instrument state.

commands.name: output_micro_learn_string
commands.description: Output Micro Learn String. Returns an 8-byte binary string describing the present CW frequency, vernier, sweep output voltage and power level, terminated with EOI. Requires CW mode with plug-in CW filter off.

commands.name: input_micro_learn_string
commands.description: Input Micro Learn String. Send 'IX' followed by the 8-byte micro learn string for rapid CW programming. Exit this binary entry mode only with the MO code, then restore displays with CW, ST, PL.

commands.name: output_mode_string
commands.description: Output Mode String. Returns an 8-byte (unpacked) binary string listing all presently active functions (no numeric values), terminated with EOI.

commands.name: output_status
commands.description: Output Status Bytes. Returns 3 sequential 8-bit status bytes (byte 1 = serial-poll status byte; bytes 2-3 = extended status), terminated with EOI. Cleared by serial poll, device clear, CS, or Instrument Preset.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clear Status Bytes. Clears all three status bytes, including the extended status bytes.

commands.name: request_status_byte_mask
commands.description: Request Status Byte Mask. Send 'RM' followed by one mask byte (decimal 0 to 255) selecting which status-byte (#1) conditions generate SRQ. Default at power-on is 0; not affected by Instrument Preset.

commands.name: request_extended_status_byte_mask
commands.description: Request Extended Status Byte Mask. Send 'RE' followed by one mask byte for the first extended status byte (#2). Default mask 255.

commands.name: request_second_extended_status_byte_mask
commands.description: Request Second Extended Status Byte Mask. Send 'R2' followed by one mask byte for the second extended status byte (#3). Default mask 255.
